# REGIME READ — 2026-07-21

## Changes Since Previous Read
- **Stage: 4 → 4 (unchanged).** No leg of the bullish trigger set completed, and no leg of the bearish escalation set fired. The regime is genuinely pinned, and this read is the first in the sequence where the internals **stabilized rather than deteriorated**.
- **THE DOMINANT FACT: VXN fell for the first time in this episode — 29.03 → 28.53 (−1.72%).** The prior read's headline was "VXN at a new episode high while VIX falls." That divergence has now **partially closed from the VXN side**, which was the specific thing the prior read named as the highest-information item to watch. VIX also fell again, 17.84 → 17.75 (−4.83% on the day). **Both vol gauges now falling together** — the first genuinely constructive vol datum of this sequence.
- **Critically, this also resolves the prior read's own timing caveat.** Last read flagged that VXN was a Jul-17 close while VIX was a Jul-20 print, so part of the divergence was an artifact. **We now have a Jul-20 VXN close (28.53) — and it came in lower.** The prior read explicitly warned "a Jul-20 VXN print could be materially lower." It was. **That caveat was correct and is now discharged.**
- **The indices stopped falling.** After two consecutive all-red, Nasdaq-led sessions, Jul 20 was **near-flat and mixed**: SPX −0.19%, Comp −0.05%, **NDX +0.04% (green — the first green NDX day of the episode)**, DJI −0.59%, Russell −0.67%. **Leadership inverted: NDX was the best index, DJI and Russell the worst.** After three sessions of "NDX worst again," this is a real change in character.
- **But the drawdown levels barely improved, and this must not be overstated.** NDX −7.0% vs 52W high (from −7.1%), Comp −6.2% (from −6.1% — actually marginally worse), SPX −2.3% (from −2.1% — worse), DJI −2.7% (from −2.1% — worse), Russell −3.4% (from −2.8% — worse). **Four of five indices are FURTHER from their highs than last read.** The one-day flat print did not repair the damage; it paused it.
- **Total score: −4 → −3.** Dealer upgrades −2 → −1 on the first VXN decline of the episode plus a second down day in VIX. Everything else holds. **This is a one-point improvement off a bearish base, not a regime change.**
- **Triggers from previous read:**
  - **Bullish — VXN closes below 25:** **NOT FIRED, but moved toward it for the first time.** 28.53, −1.72%. Still 3.5 points above the threshold.
  - **Bullish — VIX holds below 16:** **NOT FIRED.** 17.75, falling but still above 16.
  - **Bullish — NDX posts a semis-led green day:** **PARTIALLY FIRED.** NDX closed **+0.04% — green**, ending the losing streak. But +0.04% is a flat tape, not a bounce, and **no semis leadership confirmation is retrievable** — so this is scored partial, not fired.
  - **Bullish — TQQQ reclaims 200DMA (~$68):** **UNRESOLVED — no live TQQQ price again this read** (second consecutive read without a quote).
  - **Bearish — VXN closes above 30:** **CLEARED (moved away).** VXN fell to 28.53.
  - **Bearish — VIX reverses above 20 and holds:** **CLEARED (moved away).** VIX 17.75, second consecutive decline.
  - **Bearish — drawdown broadens (SPX through −5%, DJI through −4%):** **NOT FIRED, but drifting toward it.** SPX −2.3% (was −2.1%), DJI −2.7% (was −2.1%). **DJI moved 0.6pp closer to its trigger; Russell −3.4% is the weakest of the majors.** ARMED and worth watching.
  - **Bearish — TQQQ breaks 13-wk low (~$57):** **UNRESOLVED — no live price.**
  - **Bearish — HY OAS through 300 bp:** **STILL UNMEASURED. Week 13.**
- **Notable lens flips:** Dealer −2 → **−1** (first VXN decline of the episode, VIX down a second session). Cross-asset held **0** — but note the composition flipped *against* the prior read again: **Brent rebounded +1.63% to 90.67 and gold rose +1.31%**, so the oil↑+gold↑ hedge pair the last read retracted has **partially re-formed**. It is held at 0 rather than re-downgraded, precisely because this sequence has now twice over-read single-session oil moves in both directions. **Not scoring it is the discipline; flagging it is the honesty.**

> **Snapshot mix (~1-session spread):** equities + **VXN** + 10Y = **Jul 20 close**; VIX + DXY + Brent + Gold = **Jul 21 intraday.** Same structure as last read, but **the interpretive burden is much lighter this time**: the two vol gauges are now moving in the *same* direction (both down), so the timing offset no longer drives the headline. Last read's divergence story required a caveat; this read's convergence story does not depend on comparing across sessions.
>
> **Headline #1 — the vol complex turned, for the first time in this episode.** VXN −1.72% (first decline), VIX −4.83% (second decline). **This is the single most important change and it is constructive.**
>
> **Headline #2 — the Nasdaq stopped underperforming, and actually led.** NDX +0.04% (green) vs DJI −0.59% and Russell −0.67%. **Leadership inverted after three sessions of NDX-worst.**
>
> **Headline #3 — and yet four of five indices sit further from their highs than last read.** SPX −2.1%→−2.3%, DJI −2.1%→−2.7%, Russell −2.8%→−3.4%, Comp −6.1%→−6.2%. **The stabilization is one session deep and has repaired nothing.** The NDX/SPX dispersion narrowed only because SPX and DJI got worse — **not a healthy way to close a gap, and it is named as such.**
>
> **Headline #4 — the oil/gold hedge complex re-bid: Brent +1.63% to 90.67, gold +1.31%.** This is the *third* oil re-ignition attempt in four reads; the previous two both faded. **Explicitly not scored this time** — see Cross-asset lens.

## Regime Stage Tracker
- **Current Stage: 4 — Regime-change (risk-off, Nasdaq-concentrated).** Unchanged. **Stage 3 is now genuinely closer for the first time** — VXN turned down and NDX printed green — but the Stage-3 gate requires VXN below 25 (3.5 points away), VIX below 16 (1.75 away), a *semis-led* green NDX day (unconfirmed), and a TQQQ 200DMA reclaim (unpriced). **One of four ingredients is even partially in place. That is not enough to move the stage, and the temptation to reward one good session is exactly the error this sequence has already made twice on Brent.**
- **Stage Anchor:** Jul 16, 2026 — VXN re-spike above 27 + semis-led all-red tape (TSMC capex / Alphabet Gemini delay). **Unchanged; this read is the fourth session of the same episode and the first showing stabilization.**
- **Position:** Stage 4, ~4 sessions in, **stabilizing at depressed levels rather than recovering.** **Stage-4 grind (60%, unchanged):** VXN holds 25–30, VIX chops 16–20, NDX churns without either capitulating or reclaiming its trend — the snapshot fits this precisely. **Stage-3 re-stabilization (28%, up from 25%):** VXN's first decline and the green NDX print are the leading edge; needs VXN through 25 and a real semis bid, not a flat tape. **Stage-5 escalation (12%, down from 15%):** both escalation triggers (VIX>20, VXN>30) moved *away* this session. **Counterweight kept explicitly in view: DJI and Russell both deteriorated, so a broadening — not a Nasdaq — path is the live bearish risk now.**
- **Confidence: Medium.** **Held at Medium, not upgraded despite the cleaner snapshot.** Why it does not rise: (1) **still no live TQQQ price — second consecutive read**, and the instrument the entire Layer-2 analysis concerns is unpriced (the prior read's ~$67.21 IBKR print is now ~2 sessions stale and was arguably invalidated by Jul-17's move); (2) **HY OAS unmeasured for a thirteenth week** — the contained-vs-spreading discriminator remains blind, and with DJI/Russell now weakening it matters *more*, not less; (3) **one session does not establish a turn** — this sequence has twice mistaken a single-session move (Brent, in both directions) for a signal, and the same discipline must apply to a single VXN down-tick; (4) breadth internals still unretrievable, Week 13. Why it does not fall: the vol-gauge disagreement that drove last read's downgrade has resolved.
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 3):** **VXN closes below 25** AND **VIX holds below 16** AND **a genuinely semis-led green NDX day (not a flat tape)** AND **TQQQ reclaims the 200DMA (~$68).**
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 5):** **VIX reverses above 20 and holds** OR **VXN closes above 30** OR — **now the more probable path — the drawdown broadens: SPX through −5% vs 52W high, DJI through −4% (now −2.7%, moved 0.6pp closer), or Russell through −6% (now −3.4%)** OR **HY OAS confirmed through 300 bp (unmeasured Week 13).**

## Regime Verdict
US risk environment **holds Stage 4 (regime-change) with Medium confidence**, but this is the **first read of the episode where the internals improved rather than deteriorated**: **VXN fell for the first time (29.03 → 28.53, −1.72%), VIX fell a second session (17.75, −4.83%), and NDX printed its first green day (+0.04%) while leading the tape** — inverting three sessions of Nasdaq-worst leadership. The prior read's headline divergence between the two vol gauges has **converged from the VXN side**, and the prior read's explicit timing caveat — that a fresh VXN print might come in materially lower — **was correct and is now discharged.** Against that, the stabilization is **one session deep and has repaired nothing**: **four of five indices sit further from their 52-week highs than last read** (DJI −2.1%→−2.7%, Russell −2.8%→−3.4%, SPX −2.1%→−2.3%), so the NDX/SPX dispersion narrowed largely because the *rest of the tape got worse*. **The live bearish risk has therefore rotated from Nasdaq-deepening to tape-broadening.** Total improves **−4 → −3** on the Dealer upgrade alone. **Confidence is held at Medium rather than raised: a second consecutive read with no TQQQ price, a thirteenth week without credit data, and a single-session vol down-tick are not grounds for greater conviction — this sequence has already twice over-read one-day moves in Brent.**

Neutral: Positioning, Cross-asset, Sentiment, Geopolitical = 0. **Improved from −4 on the Dealer upgrade alone — a one-point move off a bearish base, driven entirely by the first VXN decline of the episode.** A VXN close below 25 with a real semis bid takes this toward −1 and Stage 3; a broadening of the drawdown into DJI/Russell takes it to −4/−5 without the Nasdaq needing to fall further |

## Diverging Signals
- **THE GOVERNING TENSION HAS ROTATED — and naming the rotation is the main analytical content of this read.** For three reads the tension was *Nasdaq deteriorating vs broad market holding*. This session **NDX went green and led while DJI (−0.59%) and Russell (−0.67%) fell**, and **DJI's gap to its 52-week high widened more than any other index (−2.1% → −2.7%)**. The bearish risk is no longer "the Nasdaq keeps breaking" — it is **"the weakness broadens out of tech into the rest of the tape."** The bearish trigger set has been rewritten accordingly.
- **The dispersion narrowed for the wrong reason, and that is stated rather than smoothed.** NDX-vs-SPX went from a 5.0pp gap to 4.7pp. That reads as healing — but NDX only improved 0.1pp while **SPX got 0.2pp worse.** The gap closed because the rest of the tape declined toward the Nasdaq, not because the Nasdaq recovered.
- **One good session vs a documented pattern of over-reading one good session.** VXN's first decline is real and is scored. But this sequence has **twice** built a thesis on a single-session Brent move and **twice** had to retract it. **The same standard is applied to VXN here: it earns a one-point Dealer upgrade, not a stage change and not a confidence upgrade.**
- **Cross-asset is the clearest live conflict, and it is being deliberately left unscored.** Brent +1.63% and gold +1.31% is a textbook risk-hedge bid that would ordinarily justify −1. It is held at 0 **because the identical signal failed twice in four reads and no named catalyst is retrievable.** This is a conscious choice to under-react, disclosed rather than hidden — if the oil bid holds a second session, the next read should downgrade.
- **Contained vs spreading — the question is now *more* urgent, and the gauge is *still* blind.** Thirteen weeks without HY OAS. With DJI and Russell now the deteriorating legs, credit spreads are exactly what would distinguish a rotation from a broadening risk-off. **The blind spot has moved from inconvenient to material.**
- **A genuine constructive that is not being suppressed:** both vol gauges fell together, both escalation triggers moved further away, and Stage-5 probability is cut 15% → 12%. The read is bearish, but **less bearish than it was**, and the total moved to say so.

## -- TQQQ INSTRUMENT ANALYSIS --

> ⚠️ **DATA-INTEGRITY NOTICE — SECOND CONSECUTIVE READ WITH NO TQQQ PRICE.** The supplied snapshot again contains **no TQQQ row**, and per the hard rules I do not invent prices. The last authoritative print remains the **~$67.21 IBKR quote from Jul 17, 2026 — now ~2 sessions stale and almost certainly invalidated**: NDX fell −1.49% on Jul 17 (≈ −4.5% at 3× leverage) and was then +0.04% on Jul 20, implying a level nearer **~$64**. **That is an inference from index moves, not a quote, and is NOT used as the analytical anchor.** Every TQQQ figure below is marked STALE or ESTIMATE. **All Layer-2 conclusions rest on VXN, NDX and structural levels — which are real, current data.** The absence of a price for two consecutive reads is the single largest limitation of this analysis and is the reason confidence is held at Medium.

## TQQQ Read
- **Momentum:** **Estimated oversold (RSI ~30–38) and now flattening rather than falling.** The ~3–4 session down-streak was broken by NDX's +0.04% print. **This is the first read of the episode where "oversold-and-stabilizing" is defensible rather than "oversold-and-still-declining"** — but it rests on one flat session, and RSI is an estimate with no current value retrievable. **Stabilizing is not the same as reversing, and nothing here evidences a reversal.**
- **Market structure: DOWNTURN — confirmed on both legs, unchanged.** Leg 1: even on the stale $67.21 anchor, spot is **−23.7% below the 4-wk high** of $88.09 — past the −20% threshold with margin (≈ −27% if the ~$64 inference holds). Leg 2: **VXN 28.53 ≥ 25**, still clearly above the line despite the decline. **Both legs satisfied. VXN would need to fall a further 3.5 points to flip leg 2 — the classification is not close to changing.** RALLY is ~+31% away and not in play.
- **Trend: below both moving averages, unchanged.** ~12% under the 50DMA ($76.39) and below the 200DMA ($68.09), broken on Jul 17. **The 200DMA and the 4-wk low ($68.08) sit together at ~+1.3% as the first overhead resistance shelf** — the level any genuine stabilization must reclaim. It has not been reclaimed.
- **Volatility & premium environment: still extreme, but expanding no longer.** VXN **28.53, the first decline of the episode**; est. TQQQ IV **~86%** (from ~87%); EM **±12.1%** (from ±12.3%). **The key change: premium stopped getting richer.** For three reads the characterization was "premium is expanding because the underlying is breaking." That mechanism paused this session. **But the level is unchanged in character — ~86% estimated IV and a ±12.1% one-week expected move remain the second-widest of the sequence and far outside a normal regime.** The vol that has already been manufactured has not drained; it merely stopped accumulating.
- **Put-selling context: HOSTILE — held, though marginally less so than last read.** The backdrop remains hostile because: (1) **DOWNTURN is confirmed on both legs with margin** — ~−23.7%+ below the 4-wk high and VXN still 3.5 points above the 25 line; (2) **the 200DMA and 4-wk low have flipped to overhead resistance at ~+1.3%**, so structure sits against any recovery and has not been reclaimed; (3) **the ±12.1% one-week expected move is the second-widest of the sequence**, and on the inferred current level its lower bound again sits essentially on the 13-wk low; (4) **no live TQQQ price for two consecutive reads** — the instrument is unpriced, which is itself a risk condition for anything strike-referenced; (5) **HY OAS unmeasured for a thirteenth week**, now more material because the deterioration has rotated into DJI and Russell. **The genuine improvements, given full weight: VXN fell for the first time, VIX fell a second session, both Stage-5 escalation triggers moved further away, the down-streak broke, and NDX led the tape.** These are why the total moved −4 → −3 and Stage-5 probability fell to 12%. **But none of them changes the structural classification: DOWNTURN, below both MAs, resistance overhead, ~86% estimated IV.** **Net: HOSTILE, one session into a stabilization that has repaired nothing and could still be noise.** Context only — no strikes, sizes, expiries, or trade calls.

## Forward Levels & Triggers
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 3 / stabilization):** **VXN closes below 25** AND **VIX holds below 16** AND **a genuinely semis-led green NDX day — not a +0.04% flat tape** AND **TQQQ reclaims the 200DMA / 4-wk-low shelf (~$68).** One ingredient is partially in place; three are not.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 5):** **the drawdown broadens — DJI through −4% vs 52W high (now −2.7%, moved 0.6pp closer this session), SPX through −5% (now −2.3%), or Russell through −6% (now −3.4%)** — **this is now the most probable escalation path** OR **VXN closes back above 30** OR **VIX reverses above 20 and holds** OR **TQQQ breaks the 13-wk low (~$57)** OR **HY OAS confirmed through 300 bp (unmeasured Week 13).**
- **Neutralizer:** **whether the VXN decline extends to a second and third session, or reverses.** One down-tick after three up sessions is not a trend, and this sequence has twice been wrong by treating a single session as one. **A second consecutive VXN decline toward 26 would make Stage 3 the live case; a reversal back above 29 would restore the deteriorating read immediately.** Watch alongside it whether DJI/Russell weakness continues — that is the channel through which this could turn worse *without* the Nasdaq falling further. The **Jul 28–29 FOMC** and the **Jul 27–31 mega-cap earnings cluster** are the next scheduled pivots.

## This Period's Catalysts
- **~Jul 21–24, 2026:** **First-wave tech earnings continue** — sentiment-relevant into an active AI-valuation derating, but **no QQQ mega-cap (Mag-7) report is confirmed inside the next 5 sessions.**
- **Jul 28–29, 2026:** **FOMC decision** (press conference Jul 29, 2:00 pm ET) — the next critical decision event, **~5 trading sessions out and sitting just outside the current window** (Jul 21, 22, 23, 24, 27). **Worth flagging: it enters the window on the very next read.**
- **Week of Jul 27–31, 2026:** **Mega-cap (Mag-7) earnings cluster** — the next major concentration-risk catalyst for NDX/TQQQ, landing directly into the AI-valuation debate driving this episode.
- **Jul 30, 2026:** Q2 GDP Advance, 8:30 am ET.
